title: Eternity in a Second: Quick-pass Continuous Authentication Using Out-ear Microphones

method.training_strategy: Supervised training using positive samples from enrolled users and equal number of negative samples from others.

evaluation.context: Best result achieved on wireless earphones during continuous authentication
Achieved during replay attack tests (E1 and E2 microphones) and spoofing attack tests (S2 3D masks)

evaluation_protocol: 80% training, 20% testing; cross-device testing with frequency response compensation

reported_limitations: Performance degrades significantly during running due to intense irregular motion
TWS earphones often have a default sampling rate of 16 kHz, which is insufficient for the 17-21 kHz ultrasound range

key_contribution: Proposed OnePiece for whole-course continuous authentication using COTS out-ear microphones
Developed a frequency-varying ultrasonic modulation scheme (ramp signal) for fast biometric extraction
Introduced a frequency response compensation strategy for cross-device one-shot registration
Implemented a wake-up mechanism to reduce power consumption
main_strength: High accuracy with very low authentication time (1s initial, 3s continuous)
Works on COTS hardware without modifications
Resilient against replay and spoofing attacks
main_weakness: Dependence on high sampling rates not available in some TWS devices
insights.notes_for_review: The system effectively bridges the gap between quick-pass initial authentication and long-term continuous monitoring using ubiquitous sensors.
